name: Hook
description:
Library component. Models a Claude Code session lifecycle hook -- one of the events that fires at an agent's session at well-defined moments (PreCompact, UserPromptSubmit, ToolUse, SessionStart). This first cut models PreCompact specifically, since that is the hook PR 745 (gastownhall/gascity#744) found a bug in.

v0.2 (defense-doc P2.4): Hook now loops up to maxCycles times, gated on observing the Agent at Active between fires. Each fire increments cycleCount via a 134/148-shape typed variable. The composing application sets maxCycles per-instance via the options override.

The coupling between Hook and other actors is intentionally loose: Hook fires whenever Agent is Active and the counter is below max. It does NOT synchronize with Patrol or any per-cycle scheduler. In real gas-town, Hook fires based on context-window pressure (a runtime/time signal), independent of patrol or substrate state. The model preserves that loose coupling so TLC explores realistic interleavings -- some traces have all patrols firing in cycle 1, some spread them across cycles, some have patrols missing their window.

AGENT.description: fires the hook event at the bound agent (the Claude session targeted by this hook). Tagged 'PreCompact' so the bound receiver can pattern-match on this specific lifecycle event.

AGENT_OBSERVE.description: observes the bound agent's state. Hook only fires PreCompact when agent is Active (the only state where the Active->Compacting receive triple is enabled).

actors.name: Hook
actors.owner: Lifecycle event firer that fires PreCompact up to maxCycles times. Single Idle state with a self-loop guarded by cycleCount < maxCycles. cycleCount is a 134-shape typed variable bound to [0, maxCycles]; assign increments it on each fire.

triples.event: lifecycle moment reached: PreCompact fires (loop entry)
